summary: Iteration 65: Phase 2 Scalability Infrastructure testing. All 12 infrastructure endpoints verified: (1) 8 infrastructure monitoring endpoints working - /api/infrastructure/health, /redis, /circuit-breakers, /circuit-breakers/{name}/reset, /events, /rate-limits, /metrics, /queues, (2) Redis token bucket rate limiting confirmed with X-RateLimit-Policy header, (3) 6 circuit breakers in closed state (aviationstack, paximum, stripe, iyzico, google_sheets, email_provider), (4) 8 rate limit tiers configured (auth_login, auth_signup, auth_password, api_global, b2b_booking, public_checkout, export, supplier_api), (5) 9 Celery queues configured (6 regular + 3 DLQ).

minor.issue: Returns empty metrics (0 bytes) - metrics not yet being recorded to observability counters. Future work needed to instrument code paths.
Returns 0 registered handlers and 0 persisted events - event handlers not yet subscribed. Future work needed to wire up event handlers.

passed_tests: GET /api/health returns {status: 'ok'} - existing health endpoint still works
GET /api/health/deep returns MongoDB collection stats - existing deep health still works
GET /api/infrastructure/health returns Redis=healthy, Celery=configured, 6 circuit breakers
GET /api/infrastructure/circuit-breakers returns 6 breakers all in 'closed' state
GET /api/infrastructure/events returns handler registry (0 handlers, 0 events currently)
GET /api/infrastructure/rate-limits returns 8 tiers: auth_login(10), auth_signup(3), auth_password(5), api_global(200), b2b_booking(30), public_checkout(10), export(5), supplier_api(60)
GET /api/infrastructure/queues returns 9 queues: default, critical, supplier, notifications, reports, maintenance, dlq.default, dlq.critical, dlq.supplier (all length=0)
critical_code_review_comments: Good: Redis token bucket implementation uses atomic Lua script for O(1) performance (~0.1ms per check)
Good: Rate limiter has MongoDB fallback if Redis unavailable - graceful degradation
Good: 6 pre-configured circuit breakers for external services with appropriate thresholds (3-10 failures)
Good: Event bus supports Redis Pub/Sub with MongoDB persistence for audit trail
Good: Celery configured with 6 regular queues + 3 DLQ for proper task routing
Good: 26 scalability indexes defined for domain_events, rate_limits, jobs, bookings, reservations, ledger_postings, settlement_runs, supplier_accruals, usage_daily, audit_log, app_cache, products
Good: TTL indexes for auto-cleanup of rate_limits, app_cache, and domain_events (30-day retention)
Good: Async tasks use exponential backoff with jitter for retry reliability
Note: Observability metrics (counters, gauges, histograms) are not yet being recorded - infrastructure is ready but instrumentation pending
Note: Event handlers are not yet subscribed - event bus ready but handlers pending wiring
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 65 verified Phase 2 Scalability Infrastructure. All /api/infrastructure/* endpoints require authentication (admin, super_admin, or agency_admin role). Redis running locally on port 6379 with 65.5% hit rate. 6 circuit breakers configured for external services (aviationstack, paximum, stripe, iyzico, google_sheets, email_provider) - all in closed state. 8 rate limit tiers using token bucket algorithm. 9 Celery queues (6 regular + 3 DLQ). Infrastructure endpoints return real-time data from Redis. X-RateLimit-Policy: token_bucket header present on API responses (not on /api/health which is explicitly skipped). Event handlers and metrics instrumentation are infrastructure-ready but not yet wired. Preview URL: https://webhook-integration-4.preview.emergentagent.com

mocked_apis.notes: All APIs use real Redis and MongoDB. No mocking in scalability infrastructure.
